zh.lifeSummary: 當Dorothy Rossell於1588年出生在英國諾廷加姆爾克倫威爾時，她的父親John Rossell21歲，母親Ellen de Rossel18歲。她於1607年12月18日與Francis Baylie在英國諾廷加姆爾內瓦爾可--特倫特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1625年10月在英國素福爾可福拉苗林加姆享年37歲，埋葬在英國素福爾可福拉苗林加姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Dorothy Rossell于1588年出生在英国諾廷加姆爾克倫威爾时，她的父亲John Rossell21岁，母亲Ellen de Rossel18岁。她于1607年12月18日与Francis Baylie在英国諾廷加姆爾內瓦爾可--特倫特结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1625年10月在英国素福爾可福拉苗林加姆享年37岁，埋葬在英国素福爾可福拉苗林加姆。

nameMeanings.name: Rossell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Anglo-Norman French personal name Ros(c)el, a diminutive of Rosce (see Ross ). Compare the double diminutive Roscelin, with which Ro(s)cel or Ros(s)el was probably freely interchangeable.
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rosel in Calvados.
English: variant of   Russell  , with which the name in 1 above was easily confused.

variants.name: Rosell
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Luis, Teobaldo, Jorge, Pedro, Santiago, Ana, Carlos, Ernesto, Jose, Miguel, Modesto, Alberto.  Scandinavian Erik, Nils.
Swedish: ornamental name composed of the element ros ‘rose’ + the suffix -ell, a derivative of the Latin adjectival ending -elius.
Catalan: variant of   Rossell  .

variants.name: Rozzell
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Rossell   or   Russell  . This surname is now rare in Britain.
Americanized form of   Rossel   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname. Compare   Rozzelle  .

variants.name: Rosselle
meanings: Altered form of English   Rossell  .
French: from the feminine form of   Rossel   and a variant of   Rousselle  . Compare   Rozelle  .

variants.name: Rosel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Felicitas, Araceli, Armando, Arturo, Cristina, Esequiel, Francisco, Margarita, Nestor, Paciano, Roberto, Rodelio.
Catalan: variant of   Rossell  .
German (Rösel): from a pet form of Rose 1.

variants.name: Ross
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from one or other of a number of Scottish and English places called Ross or Roos(e), especially Roose (Lancashire) and Roos (East Yorkshire). The placenames derive from a British ancestor of Welsh rhos ‘moor, heath, plain’, which is the sense of the cognate Gaelic word ros. The Scottish surname has at least three origins. First, the Anglo-Norman family from Roos (East Yorkshire) was introduced to Scotland when Robert of Roos, lord of Wark Castle (Northumberland), married Isabella, an illegitimate daughter of King William the Lion. Second, various families took the name from the province of Ross in northern Scotland and other places of that name. Third, there were Norman families in Scotland by the 13th century who probably derived their name from Rots in Normandy (see 2 below). The descendants of Godfrey de Ros, tenant of the de Morville lords of Cunninghame, were major landholders in Ayrshire, and almost certainly took their name from Rots. The Rose family of Kilravock (Nairnshire) may take their name from either of these three (see Rose ). The lairds of Balnagown adopted the surname Ross after the earldom of Ross (to which they considered themselves rightful heirs) had passed into other hands through the female line.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rots in Calvados (France), probably named with the ancient Germanic element rod ‘clearing’ (compare Rhodes ). This was the original home of a family de Ros, who were established in Kent in 1130 and had major estates in Kent until well into the 13th century.

English: habitational name from Wrose, in Shipley, near Bradford (Yorkshire), with re-spelling of Wr- as R- due to the loss of /w/ before /r/ in early modern English pronunciation. The spelling Wrose is no longer current. The placename derives from Old English wrāse ‘knot, something twisted’, referring to the steep-sided hill on which the settlement stands, with the sense ‘broken or twisting hill’.

variants.name: Rossel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Fernando, Juan, Alvaro, Ana, Carols, Catarino, Guillermo, Javier, Jose, Orlando, Pedro, Ruben.  French Philippe, Yves.  Dutch Gerrit, Flor.
French, Dutch, and Flemish: nickname for a red-haired man, from a diminutive of Old French and Dutch ros ‘red’.
Hispanic: probably an altered form of Catalan   Rossell  .

nameMeanings.name: Dorothy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Usual English form of   Dorothea .  The name was not used in the Middle Ages, but was taken up in the 15th century and became common thereafter. It was borne by the American film star  Dorothy Lamour  (  1914–1996 , born  Dorothy Kaumeyer ).

variants.name: Dorothee
meanings: Usual English form of   Dorothea .  The name was not used in the Middle Ages, but was taken up in the 15th century and became common thereafter. It was borne by the American film star  Dorothy Lamour  (  1914–1996 , born  Dorothy Kaumeyer ).

variants.name: Dorothie
meanings: Usual English form of   Dorothea .  The name was not used in the Middle Ages, but was taken up in the 15th century and became common thereafter. It was borne by the American film star  Dorothy Lamour  (  1914–1996 , born  Dorothy Kaumeyer ).

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Dorothy Rossell was born in 1588, in Cromwell, Nottinghamshire, England, her father, John Rossell, was 21 and her mother, Ellen de Rossel, was 18. She married Francis Baylie on 18 December 1607, in Newark-on-Trent, Nottinghamshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died in October 1625, in Framlingham, Suffolk, England, at the age of 37, and was buried in Framlingham, Suffolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Dorothy Rossell est née en 1588, à Cromwell, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, John Rossell, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Ellen de Rossel, avait 18 ans. Elle a épousé Francis Baylie le 18 décembre 1607, à Newark-on-Trent, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en octobre 1625, à Framlingham, Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 37 ans, et a été enterrée à Framlingham, Suffolk, Angleterre.

Dezember 1607 in Newark-on-Trent, Nottinghamshire, England Francis Baylie geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb im Oktober 1625 im Alter von 37 Jahren in Framlingham, Suffolk, England und wurde in Framlingham, Suffolk, England begraben.

Lei sposato Francis Baylie il 18 dicembre 1607, nel Newark-on-Trent, Nottinghamshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. ja.lifeSummary: Dorothy Rossellが1588年にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアクロムウエルで生まれた時，父John Rossellは21歳，母Ellen de Rosselは18歳であった。1607年12月18日にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアニューアルク-オン-トレントでFrancis Baylieと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1625年10月にイギリススフォルクフラムリンガムで37歳で死去し，イギリススフォルクフラムリンガムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Dorothy Rossell(이)가 1588년 영국 노틴그함실르 크롬웰에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Rossell(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ellen de Rossel(은)는 18세였다. 그녀는 1607년 12월 18일 영국 노틴그함실르 뉴아르k--트렌트에서 Francis Baylie(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1625년 10월 영국 수폴크 프램링함에서 37세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수폴크 프램링함에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Dorothy Rossell nasceu em 1588, em Cromwell, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Rossell, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Ellen de Rossel, tinha 18 anos. Ela casou-se com Francis Baylie em 18 de dezembro de 1607, em Newark-on-Trent, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em outubro de 1625, em Framlingham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, com 37 anos, e foi sepultada em Framlingham, Suffolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Dorothy Rossell родилась в 1588 г. в местности кромвелл, ноттингхамшир, Англия, её отцу по имени John Rossell было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Ellen de Rossel, 18. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Francis Baylie 18 декабря 1607 г. в местности нюарк-он-трент, ноттингхамшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла октябрь 1625 г. в местности фрамлингхам, суффолк, Англия в возрасте 37 лет и была похоронена в местности фрамлингхам, суффолк, Англия.
